data.title: THE SEQUENCE
blocks: Stage 1 — /v0.
Stage 2 — /v1.
Stage 3 — SDD.
Stage 4 — Boondoggle Score.
Stage 5 — Build execution.

data.title: WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE.
blocks: What did you ask Claude? (Or for human rows — what did you set out to produce?)
What did Claude return? (Or what did you produce?)
· What did you do with the return? Accept as-is, accept with edits, reject and respecify, rewind?
· Which supervisory capacity governed the decision?

data.title: THE POST-BUILD DOCUMENT.
blocks: Section 1 — What I built.
Section 2 — What I delegated to Claude and why.
Section 3 — What I kept for myself and why.
Section 4 — What I learned that I didn't know before.
Section 5 — What I would do differently.

data.title: THE SIX DELIVERABLES.
blocks: v0.md one-sentence problem formulation. One sentence, one user, one outcome. Gate 1 satisfied.
sdd.md minimum viable SDD. Five sections, each naming at least one concrete artifact. Gate 3 satisfied.
score.md Boondoggle Score for Phase 1. Three to seven rows, all five columns, every handoff condition sharp. Gate 4 satisfied.
execution-log.md — one entry per row, written during the build. Each entry the specification you ran, the handoff condition evaluation with result, the supervisory capacity label. Include any row
verification.md — all three passes, in order, against the running build. For Pass 3 the user needs sentences read against the build, with a per-sentence verdict.

data.title: WHERE NEXT
blocks: The book ends with this chapter. The practice does not.
